---
title: "Political Affiliation Prediction"
author: "James Compagno"
format: 
  dashboard:
    theme: flatly
    orientation: columns
    embed-resources: true
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import plotnine as p9
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import (
    accuracy_score, confusion_matrix, roc_auc_score,
    precision_score, recall_score, f1_score
)
from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV, cross_val_predict
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
import warnings
import os
from sklearn.exceptions import ConvergenceWarning

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=ConvergenceWarning)
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning)
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
os.environ["QUARTO_PYTHON"] = "1"
```

```{python}
#| label: load-data
#| include: false

CAH_train = pd.read_csv("/Users/jamescompagno/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Personal/MSBA/GSB_544/Final_Project/gsb-544-fall-2025-classification/CAH-201803-train.csv")
CAH_test = pd.read_csv("/Users/jamescompagno/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Personal/MSBA/GSB_544/Final_Project/gsb-544-fall-2025-classification/CAH-201803-test.csv")

X = CAH_train.drop(["id_num", "political_affiliation"], axis=1)
y = CAH_train["political_affiliation"]

X_test = CAH_test.drop("id_num", axis=1)
test_ids = CAH_test["id_num"]

numerical_features = ["Q2", "Q15", "Q16", "Q17"] 
categorical_features = ["Q1", "Q4", "Q5", "Q6", "Q7", "Q8", "Q9", 
                        "Q10", "Q11", "Q12", "Q13", "Q14", "Q18"]

ct = ColumnTransformer(
    [
        ("standardize", StandardScaler(), numerical_features),
        ("encode", OneHotEncoder(drop='first', sparse_output=False), categorical_features)
    ],
    verbose_feature_names_out=False,
).set_output(transform="pandas")
```

```{python}
#| label: train-model
#| include: false

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("preprocess", ct),
    ("logistic", LogisticRegression(solver='saga', max_iter=5000, random_state=67))
])

param_grid = {
    'logistic__C': [0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 100],
    'logistic__penalty': ['l1', 'l2'],
    'logistic__class_weight': [None, 'balanced']
}

grid_search = GridSearchCV(pipe, param_grid, cv=5, scoring='accuracy', n_jobs=-1)
grid_search.fit(X, y)

best_model = grid_search.best_estimator_
best_params = grid_search.best_params_

y_pred_cv = cross_val_predict(best_model, X, y, cv=5)
y_proba_cv = cross_val_predict(best_model, X, y, cv=5, method='predict_proba')

cv_accuracy = accuracy_score(y, y_pred_cv)
cv_roc_auc = roc_auc_score(y, y_proba_cv, multi_class='ovr', average='weighted')
cv_precision = precision_score(y, y_pred_cv, average='weighted', zero_division=0)
cv_recall = recall_score(y, y_pred_cv, average='weighted', zero_division=0)
cv_f1 = f1_score(y, y_pred_cv, average='weighted', zero_division=0)
conf_matrix = confusion_matrix(y, y_pred_cv)
```

# Overview

## Row {height=20%}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "CV Accuracy"
dict(
    value=f"{cv_accuracy:.1%}",
    color="success"
)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "CV Percision"
dict(
    value=f"{cv_precision:.1%}",
    color="primary"
)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "ROC AUC"
dict(
    value=f"{cv_roc_auc:.3f}",
    color="info"
)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "F1 Score"
dict(
    value=f"{cv_f1:.3f}",
    color="secondary"
)
```

## Row {height=40%}

### Column {width=50%}

```{python}
#| title: "Target Distribution"
#| output: true

target_counts = y.value_counts().reset_index()
target_counts.columns = ['Political_Affiliation', 'Count']

(p9.ggplot(target_counts, p9.aes(x='Political_Affiliation', y='Count', fill='Political_Affiliation'))
    + p9.geom_col(width=0.6)
    + p9.geom_text(p9.aes(label='Count'), va='bottom', size=10)
    + p9.labs(x='', y='Count')
    + p9.theme(figure_size=(5, 4))
    + p9.theme(
        panel_background=p9.element_rect(fill="white"), 
        panel_grid_major=p9.element_line(color="#f2f7f8", size=0.5),
        legend_position='none'
    )
    + p9.scale_fill_manual(values={"Democrat":"#3498db", "Republican":"#e74c3c", "Independent":"#2ecc71"})
).draw()
```

### Column {width=50%}

```{python}
#| title: "Confusion Matrix"
#| output: true

classes = sorted(y.unique())
cm_df = pd.DataFrame(conf_matrix, index=classes, columns=classes)

cm_long = cm_df.reset_index().melt(id_vars='index', var_name='Predicted', value_name='Count')
cm_long.columns = ['Actual', 'Predicted', 'Count']

(p9.ggplot(cm_long, p9.aes(x='Predicted', y='Actual', fill='Count'))
    + p9.geom_tile(color='white', size=2)
    + p9.geom_text(p9.aes(label='Count'), size=12, color='white')
    + p9.scale_fill_gradient(low='#3498db', high='#2c3e50')
    + p9.labs(x='Predicted', y='Actual')
    + p9.theme(figure_size=(5, 4))
    + p9.theme(
        panel_background=p9.element_rect(fill="white"), 
        panel_grid_major=p9.element_line(color="#f2f7f8", size=0.5),
        legend_position='none'
    )
).draw()
```

## Row {height=40%}

### Column {width=50%}

**Model Summary**

- Logistic Regression with L1/L2 regularization
- 5-fold cross-validation for robust evaluation
- Multi-class classification (Democrat, Republican, Independent)

**Best Parameters Found**

- Regularization strength (C) and penalty type optimized via grid search
- Class weighting considered to handle any imbalance

**Business Takeaways**

- Model achieves ~62.1% accuracy in predicting political affiliation from survey responses
- The final model had the highest accuracy and Roc Auc of all 16 different Corss-Validated Models
- The model predicts Independents best with 30 correct classifications, and Democrats worst with only 5 correct classifications.

### Column {width=50%}

```{python}
#| title: "Best Hyperparameters"

params_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Parameter': ['C (Regularization)', 'Penalty', 'Class Weight'],
    'Best Value': [
        best_params['logistic__C'],
        best_params['logistic__penalty'],
        str(best_params['logistic__class_weight'])
    ]
})

params_df
```
